claim: For a 16777216-element fp32 SAXPY on AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT gfx1201, float4 vectorized loads/stores measured only 1.012440x faster than the scalar baseline using HIP-event timing; distributions overlap, so this is near-neutral evidence rather than a broad vectorization win.
expected_profiler_signals: similar memory bandwidth between scalar and float4 paths
curation_note: timing-only near-neutral: HIP-event timings and correctness passed, but rocprofiler/rocprof counters were not collected and the scalar/float4 timing distributions overlap. Treat this as a cautionary vectorization example, not an architecture-general win.
